---
title: "Java Learning"
author: "Tao Zou"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    highlight: tango
---

```{r include=FALSE}
setwd("D:/Java_workspace/Java_Learning/")
```

# class literal
```{java}
public class Main {
  public int a = 32;
  public void mymethod() {System.out.println("Yes");}
  
  public static void main(String[] args) {
    Class<Main> mainclass = Main.class;
    System.out.println("The class name is: " + mainClass.getName());
    System.out.println("If the class is interface: " + mainClass.isInterface());
    
    try {
      System.out.println("Public field 'a': " + mainClass.getField("a"));
    } catch (NoSuchFieldException e) {
      System.out.println("File not found.");
    } catch (SecurityException e) {
      System.out.println("Access to the field is denied.");
    }
    try {
      System.out.println("Public method 'mymethod': " + mainClass.getMethod("mymethod"));
    } catch (NoSuchMethodException e) {
      System.out.println("Method not found.");
    } catch (SecurityException e) {
      System.out.println("Access to the method is denied.")
    }
  }
}
```

# record
`record` is a special `class`. In the code below, `id` and `content` will be \<private\> and \<final\> fields. 

```{java}
// Main.java
record Greeting(long id, String content) {}

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        Greeting greeting = new Greeting(256, "Hello, World.");
        System.out.println(greeting.id() + " " + greeting.content());
    }
}
```

# java.util
## Optional
```{java}
import java.util.Optional

// Create an Optional instance that contains a value. And print it.
Optional<String> optioanlWithValue = Optional.of("Hello, World");
optionalWithValue.ifPresent(System.out::println);

// Create an empty Optional instance. And give it a value.
Optional<String> emptyOptional = Optional.empty();
String
```
